title: The Ballad of the Emeu
content:
В В В В Oh, say, have you seen at the Willows so green
В В В В So charming and rurally true
В В В В A singular bird, with a manner absurd,
В В В В Which they call the Australian Emeu?
В В В В Have you
В В В В Ever seen this Australian Emeu?

В В В В It trots all around with its head on the ground,
В В В В Or erects it quite out of your view;
В В В В And the ladies all cry, when its figure they spy,
В В В В пїЅOh! what a sweet pretty Emeu!
В В В В Oh! do
В В В В Just look at that lovely Emeu!пїЅ

В В В В One day to this spot, when the weather was hot,
В В В В Came Matilda Hortense Fortescue;
В В В В And beside her there came a youth of high name,
В В В В Augustus Florell Montague:
В В В В The two
В В В В Both loved that wild, foreign Emeu.

В В В В With two loaves of bread then they fed it, instead
В В В В Of the flesh of the white Cockatoo,
В В В В Which once was its food in that wild neighborhood
В В В В Where ranges the sweet Kangaroo,
В В В В That too
В В В В Is game for the famous Emeu!

В В В В Old saws and gimlets but its appetite whets,
В В В В Like the world-famous bark of Peru;
В В В В ThereпїЅs nothing so hard that the bird will discard,
В В В В And nothing its taste will eschew
В В В В That you
В В В В Can give that long-legged Emeu!

В В В В The time slipped away in this innocent play,
В В В В When up jumped the bold Montague:
В В В В пїЅWhereпїЅs that specimen pin that I gayly did win
В В В В In raffle, and gave unto you,
В В В В Fortescue?пїЅ
В В В В No word spoke the guilty Emeu!

В В В В пїЅQuick! tell me his name whom thou gavest that same,
В В В В Ere these hands in thy blood I imbrue!пїЅ
В В В В пїЅNay, dearest,пїЅ she cried, as she clung to his side,
В В В В пїЅIпїЅm innocent as that Emeu!пїЅ
В В В В пїЅAdieu!пїЅ
В В В В He replied, пїЅMiss M. H. Fortescue!пїЅ

В В В В Down she dropped at his feet, all as white as a sheet,
В В В В As wildly he fled from her view;
В В В В He thought пїЅtwas her sin, for he knew not the pin
В В В В Had been gobbled up by the Emeu;
В В В В All through
В В В В The voracity of that Emeu!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Bret Harte (Francis) represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Bret Harte (Francis)'s bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
